2.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Strongly bacteriophage-dominated (vast majority of reads phage); eukaryotic viruses minor (~10-25%).
2.plausibility_verdict: MATCH with one top-family flag. Phage dominance (66%) correct direction (arguably low side); DNA-only correct. Flag: top family Microviridae, but saliva viromes are dominated by tailed Caudovirales (Siphoviridae), not Microviridae.

citations.title: Evidence of a robust resident bacteriophage population revealed through analysis of the human salivary virome.
citations.supports: Healthy saliva viral communities dominated by bacteriophages; DNA virome.

citations.title: Altered oral viral ecology in association with periodontal disease.
citations.supports: Saliva and oral biofilm viromes composed largely of bacteriophage; predators of bacteria dominate.

4.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Eukaryote-dominated, led by Anelloviridae; bacteriophages present but comparatively low in health.
4.expected_na_type: Predominantly DNA (Anelloviridae ssDNA); small RNA fraction (rhinovirus).
4.plausibility_verdict: MISMATCH (balance inverted + wrong top taxon). Observed phage 50 > euk 35 (phage-dominated); healthy nasopharynx is eukaryote/Anellovirus-dominated. Top family should be Anelloviridae, not a phage family (Aliceevansviridae). Caveat: strongest evidence pediatric/asthma cohorts, but Anellovirus dominance robust.

citations.title: Metagenomic analysis of viral genetic diversity in respiratory samples from children with severe acute respiratory infection in China.
citations.supports: Respiratory virome of children without severe infection dominated by Anelloviridae.

citations.title: Respiratory eukaryotic virome expansion and bacteriophage deficiency characterize childhood asthma.
citations.supports: Nasopharyngeal virome dominated by eukaryotic viruses; Anelloviridae most abundant family; bacteriophages low.

citations.title: Characterization of the nasopharyngeal viral microbiome from children with community-acquired pneumonia but negative for Luminex xTAG respiratory viral panel assay detection.
citations.supports: Nasopharyngeal virome dominated by eukaryotic respiratory viruses (Paramyxo/Herpes/Anello/Polyoma).

12.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Substantial phage (Pseudomonas/Staphylococcus) plus notable eukaryotic (herpes/retro); majority of viral diversity uncharacterized.
12.disease_signature: Enrichment of Pseudomonas/Staphylococcus phages; eukaryotic fraction dominated by herpesviruses and retroviruses; large dark-matter fraction.
12.plausibility_verdict: MATCH. phage 43%, euk 24%, unknown-fam 33% all supported (Willner 2009: majority of CF viral diversity uncharacterized). DNA-dominant with RNA present consistent. Best-supported respiratory site.

citations.title: Metagenomic analysis of respiratory tract DNA viral communities in cystic fibrosis and non-cystic fibrosis individuals.
citations.supports: Majority of CF sputum viral diversity uncharacterized; eukaryotic communities dominated by herpesviruses and retroviruses.

citations.title: Deciphering the role of phage in the cystic fibrosis airway.
citations.supports: CF-associated phage share a common core metabolism reflecting disease state (secondary support).

13.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Eukaryotic RNA viruses overwhelmingly dominate; bacteriophages ~0 (RNA phages rare/not captured).
13.expected_na_type: RNA-dominated by design; residual DNA fraction expected.
13.plausibility_verdict: MATCH on structure (lower confidence sourcing). euk 87%, RNA 72%, phage ~0 correct; Orthomyxoviridae defensible top (rhinovirus often equally prevalent). DNA 15% acceptable as residual. Caveat: primary source (Minor 2023) is air-sampling, confirms families circulate but not healthy-host composition.

citations.title: Metagenomic sequencing detects human respiratory and enteric viruses in air samples collected from congregate settings.
citations.supports: Detects influenza A/C, RSV, human coronaviruses, rhinovirus, SARS-CoV-2 as expected respiratory RNA families (families circulate; not a healthy-host composition study).

19.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Eukaryote-dominated, led by Anelloviridae/TTV; phages of respiratory bacteria secondary.
19.plausibility_verdict: MISMATCH on top taxon and unknown fraction (euk-dominance direction correct). euk 47 > phage 20 correct; but top family Unknown where Anelloviridae expected; unknown-fam 33.6% too high and suspiciously near-identical to CF 33.4% (shared-default tell).

citations.title: Viral metagenomics reveal blooms of anelloviruses in the respiratory tract of lung transplant recipients.
citations.supports: All BAL samples (including healthy controls) contained anelloviruses; anelloviruses define the respiratory DNA virome.

citations.title: The Perioperative Lung Transplant Virome: Torque Teno Viruses Are Elevated in Donor Lungs and Show Divergent Dynamics in Primary Graft Dysfunction.
citations.supports: Lung/BAL virome mainly Torque teno viruses (Anellovirus) and bacteriophages of respiratory bacteria.

citations.title: Lower Airway Virology in Health and Disease-From Invaders to Symbionts.
citations.supports: Review: lower airway virome includes Anelloviridae, Herpesviridae, bacteriophages (secondary support).
